shell.source:
guarded-app
Console Users Danger zone

pages/home.source:
Guards demo

@if (user) {

Signed in as {{ user }}. Log out

} @else {

You are anonymous. Try the console — its guard bounces you to the login page.

}
  • /admin — requireAuth
  • /admin/users — inherits requireAuth from the parent route
  • /admin/danger — requireAuth, then requireAdmin (async): bob is bounced to /denied

pages/denied.source:
Not allowed

requireAdmin bounced you: you are signed in, but not admin.

Become admin · Home

pages/admin.source:
Console

Welcome, {{ user }} — requireAuth let you in.

  • Users (guarded by inheritance)
  • Danger zone (admin only)

Log out

pages/admin-users.source:
Users

You reached a child route — its requireAuth came from the parent admin route, not from this one.

    @for (m of members; track m) {
  • {{ m }}
    • }

pages/admin-danger.source:
Danger zone

Only {{ user }} sees this: the chain was requireAuth (inherited from admin) then requireAdmin (this route's own, async).

pages/login.source:
Log in

A guard sent you here. Pick a user — /go/login/:user is an action route: its guard signs you in and redirects to the console (it never renders).

Log in as admin · Log in as bob
